records.ety: [OE. deierie, from deie, daie, maid; of Scand. origin; cf. Icel. deigja maid, dairymaid, Sw. deja, orig., a baking maid, fr. Icel. deig. √66. See Dough.]

defs: The place, room, or house where milk is kept, and converted into butter or cheese.
quotes: What stores my dairies and my folds contain.

defs: That department of farming which is concerned in the production of milk, and its conversion into butter and cheese.
quotes: Grounds were turned much in England either to feeding or dairy; and this advanced the trade of English butter.

notes: ☞ Dairy is much used adjectively or in combination; as, dairy farm, dairy countries, dairy house or dairyhouse, dairyroom, dairywork, etc.
